15MIN INCREMENTS

Scripts: Module 1
SCRIPTS
TO SET THE FRAME
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 1 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
MODULE 1 SCRIPTS:
The following pages provide tested scripts to enter difficult business situations
and achieve frame control. For your own practice, it is recommended that
you prepare scripts in advance. Then, when you recognize the situation that
requires frame control, you will have a response ready.
The Let’s Jump In Routine
Category: Time Reframe
Key ingredients: Defiance and light humor
Difficulty: Beginner
ROUTINE:
TARGET: 	Hey guys, thanks for coming in. We thought we’d spend a few minutes to tell you about ourselves as a
firm and our history and a little bit about our partners.
YOU: 	Hey, look, why don’t we get started on pitching our deal? OK, then you can tell us about yourselves in
context of what we have.
The Teaser Routine
Category: Power Frame Disrupter
Key ingredients: Defiance and light humor
Difficulty: Beginner
ROUTINE:
1. Place a folder on the conference table labeled “Confidential—John Smith”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
MODULE 1 SCRIPTS:
2. When target reaches for it, smile, take it back ---
YOU: 	One second, not yet. You have to wait for this.
The I’ve Only Got A Second Reframe Routine
Category: Time reframe
Key ingredients: Inner authenticity
Difficulty: High
ROUTINE:
TARGET: 	Hi, I’ve only got [whatever time] to meet with you, let’s make it quick.
YOU: 	Tell you what, I don’t work like that. We could reschedule, but there’s no sense in that unless
we like each other and trust each other. I need to know --- are you good to work with, can you keep
appointments, and stick to a schedule?
The Compete for My Attention Routine
Category: Prize frame
Key ingredients: Inner authenticity
Difficulty: High
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
MODULE 1 SCRIPTS:
ROUTINE:
YOU: 	[make sure you’re smiling and doing the following with an exaggerated weary tone] So many buyers,
yet only one of me. How are you going to compete for my attention.
The How I Got Here Routine
Category: Introducing Yourself
Key ingredients: Big win highlights
Difficulty: Medium
ROUTINE:
TARGET: 	What’s your background? / How’d you end up doing this?
YOU: 	[Deliver a three-point stack of successes - example here]:
1. My degree is from Berkeley, and I did my MBA at UCLA.
2. After that I was at Mckinsey for four years, where I developed a sales program for Lexus that saved
them about $15 million and which they still use today.
3. I left consulting six months ago to work on this Big Idea.
The Analyst Frame Crusher Routine
Category: Analyst Frame Disrupter
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
MODULE 1 SCRIPTS:
Key ingredients: Deep inner authenticity
Difficulty: High
ROUTINE:
TARGET: 	Hey, we’d like to get some more detail on these revenue contracts. You know, what are the milestone
payments that the customer is making to you?
YOU: 	Hey, look I’ve got about 20 minutes here to show you who we are and the major components of the
deal. The product, our competitive advantage, and what we do, and who we do it for. We would not
be here today if the revenue contracts did not have teeth, cause we know we would blow out in due
diligence.
So let’s stay focused on the big idea, and when it gets to it, all the details are in the binder. And look,
if you blow out in due diligence, I have no problem. But let’s, at least, decide if you like me, and if you
like the deal, and the product. Because until we’re there, there’s no point in getting into the details.
I promise you it’s in a binder. We would not have invested our time to come here with ginned up
information. Let’s focus on the chemistry between us, whether you like us as a team, the product, and
the market size. That’s what we can accomplish in the twenty minutes we have.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 5 --
